Healthcare Uncharted · Off the Map

The Secret Menu

You found it. Eight taps on the compass, or you just knew the address. Either way, welcome to the part of the site that isn't on the site. No nav link points here. The sitemap doesn't list it. Search engines are told to look away.

What this is
A back room. Half-finished ideas, experiments that aren't ready for the front page, and the occasional thing that's just for people paying attention. It works like Learn. It's just quieter. Check back, the menu changes.
Game
Uncharted General
A healthcare systems roguelite. Run a hospital network quarter by quarter. Set pay, move staff, open units, then run the quarter and see if you hit the goal. Clear it, pick an upgrade. Miss it, the run's over. It gets meaner every level.
Playable · v1 →
Field Guide
Laws & Paradoxes
The recurring patterns that shape healthcare and health IT. Already live in Learn, but it earns a spot here too. Start with Goodhart, then go find where you've watched it happen.
Live · Learn →
Vision
Camp Nauvoo
A long-term plan to buy an 86-acre camp in the Sierra foothills and run it as a nonprofit: respite for burned-out clinicians, last good days outdoors for people near the end of life, and public camping that keeps it all running.
The long game →
NotesDraft
Raw Field Notes
The stuff that doesn't fit a clean article yet. Observations from the build, the ward, the rollout that went sideways. Unpolished on purpose.
In the back room ·
ExperimentDraft
Something That Isn't Ready
A placeholder for the next thing. When it's worth showing, it lands here first.
Coming together ·
A living resource for clinical informatics, healthcare data architecture, and the real story behind the systems that run modern medicine.

Built by David Eitel, RRT, MHA, MSRT, RRT-ACCS

Source Policy
All vendor-specific terminology, technical specifications, and system references on this site are derived from publicly available documentation — including fhir.epic.com, fhir.cerner.com, HL7 FHIR R4, ONC HealthIT.gov, and HHS.gov. Each factual claim is linked to its source. This site does not reproduce proprietary materials, internal vendor training content, or licensed documentation.
